params.requiredCredentialType: Credential type hash required by this rule

returns.valid: Whether the attestation is current and compliant
Whether the credential meets the eIDAS requirement

commitReport(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,str.details: The actual report data is stored off-chain. Only the hash is on-chain      for auditability and non-repudiation.

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

params.attestationHash: Hash of the full attestation data (off-chain)
params.satisfiedRules: Array of rule IDs satisfied by this attestation

params.issuerHash: Hash of the qualified trust service provider (QTSP)
params.trustedListHash: Hash of the EU Trusted List entry for the issuer
params.validityPeriod: How long the marker is valid

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

screenSanctions(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32[.details: Uses a Merkle proof for privacy: the full list is never on-chain.

params.merkleProof: Merkle proof (if subject IS on the list)

returns.hit: True if the subject is sanctioned

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

params.listSize: Number of entries in the list
params.merkleRoot: New Merkle root of the sanctions list

addComplianceRule(bytes32,bytes32,string.notice: Add a compliance rule to a jurisdiction.

checkCompliance(bytes32,bytes32).notice: Check compliance status of a credential in a jurisdiction.

checkEIDAS(bytes32,uint8).notice: Check if a credential has a valid eIDAS marker at or above a given level.

commitReport(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,str.notice: Commit a regulatory report hash on-chain.

issueComplianceAttestation(bytes32,bytes.notice: Issue a compliance attestation for a credential in a jurisdiction.

markEIDASCredential(bytes32,uint8,bytes3.notice: Mark a credential with eIDAS 2.0 assurance level.

recordTravelRuleTransfer(bytes32,bytes32.notice: Record a travel rule compliant cross-border transfer.

screenSanctions(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32[.notice: Screen a subject against a jurisdiction's sanctions list.

updateSanctionsList(bytes32,bytes32,uint.notice: Update the sanctions list Merkle root for a jurisdiction.
